code-input-1.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

sketch-1.title: What are String Functions?
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown: String functions in SQL let you manipulate text data in your queries. Common examples include changing case (like UPPER or LOWER), finding the length of a string (LENGTH), or combining text (using || or CONCAT). These are especially useful when working with columns like names or titles.

sketch-2.title: Using String Functions in SELECT
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown:
You can apply string functions directly in your SELECT statements. For example, to display all student names in uppercase from the students table:

```sql
SELECT UPPER(name) AS name_uppercase FROM students;
```

This returns a column with all names in uppercase letters.

sketch-3.title: Combining Strings
sketch-3.bodyMarkdown:
To join (concatenate) two or more strings in SQLite, use the `||` operator. For example, to show a student's name with their grade level:

```sql
SELECT name || ' (Grade ' || grade_level || ')' AS student_info FROM students;
```

This creates a new column like 'Mia (Grade 9)'.
